summary.intent: Address five Codex review findings on a menu component PR (#672) in the ars-ui repo, using a TDD approach to patch submenu RTL arrows, prop-derived context sync, focus flag clearing on action close, wall-clock typeahead timing, and stale checkbox/radio state after item updates.
summary.outcome: All five regression tests were written, confirmed failing, then made green with minimal production changes. Formatting and clippy passed. A follow-up commit was pushed to the same PR branch with only the menu file staged.

repo: The ars-components crate uses nightly rustfmt; fmt differences must be resolved before committing even when logic is correct.
Locale direction (RTL/LTR) lives in ars-i18n and must be consulted by menu key handlers for correct ArrowLeft/ArrowRight submenu navigation.
Prop-derived cached state (ids, loop_focus) must be explicitly re-synced in on_props/props_changed; the menu did not do this automatically.
Typeahead timing falls back to wall-clock (SystemTime/Instant) when no timestamp is provided; the previous implementation did not apply this fallback consistently.
Stale checkbox/radio selection state must be cleared when UpdateItems is processed, not lazily on next interaction.
Snapshot tests are run via `cargo insta test` and must be checked before committing menu changes.
Property-based state machine tests for the menu are run with `--ignored` flag via `cargo test proptest_state_machines`.
An unrelated ars-dioxus working-tree change was present during the session and deliberately left unstaged.

code.finding: Central file for all five fixes: RTL submenu arrows, prop sync, focus-on-close clearing, wall-clock typeahead fallback, and stale selection state after UpdateItems.

workflow: TDD loop: write failing regression tests first, confirm they fail against current code, then apply minimal production fix until tests go green.
PR label `snapshot-reviewed` must be added and `@codex review` must be posted as a comment to trigger the Codex bot; reactions (👀) indicate the bot has checked out the review.
Codex review state is polled via GitHub API reactions on the triggering comment, combined with GraphQL queries for unresolved review threads.
CI check progression (Clippy → Workspace Check → Unit Tests) can be tracked via `gh pr view --json statusCheckRollup`.
Stage only the intentionally changed files before committing to avoid pulling in unrelated working-tree diffs.

friction: Codex review required multiple long polling intervals (60s, 120s, 120s, 180s, 180s+) before findings appeared, blocking progress for ~12 minutes.
Nightly fmt introduced a wrapping difference that was only caught after all logic checks passed, requiring an extra fmt application and re-verification round.
Four iterative apply_patch + test cycles were needed to get all five fixes green, suggesting the fixes were not all correct on the first attempt.
open_items: The unrelated ars-dioxus working-tree change was intentionally left unstaged and is not addressed in this PR.
Codex review final state (approved vs. pending further comments) was not confirmed in the transcript before the follow-up commit was pushed.
